title: 10 best herbal essential oils for stress
intro_desc: Herbal essential oils for stress are natural remedies extracted from plants, which help alleviate anxiety and promote relaxation by reducing cortisol levels and calming the mind. The best herbal essential oils for stress include Lavandula angustifolia, Valeriana officinalis, Melissa officinalis, Passiflora incarnata, Anthemis nobilis, Santalum album, Avena sativa, Boswellia serrata, Matricaria chamomilla, and Bacopa monnieri. If left untreated, stress can negatively impact lives by leading to insomnia, irritability, decreased productivity, and strained relationships, ultimately affecting overall well-being and quality of life.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: English lavender essential oils helps with stress because its calming scent has a profound impact on the mind and body. When inhaled, it activates the parasympathetic nervous system, slowing down heart rate and blood pressure, and promoting relaxation. The oil's sweet, floral aroma also reduces feelings of anxiety and tension, allowing individuals to unwind and destress. As a result, English lavender essential oils can be used as a natural stress-reliever, providing a sense of calm and tranquility in the midst of chaos.
remedy_properties: Anxiolytic (Anxiety-reducing) Property: English Lavender essential oil has been shown to have a calming effect on the nervous system, helping to reduce anxiety and promote relaxation.
Sedative Property: The sedative properties of English Lavender essential oil can help to calm the mind and body, reducing stress and promoting a good night's sleep.

Adaptogenic Property: English Lavender essential oil has adaptogenic properties that allow it to help the body adapt to stress and promote balance in the nervous system, reducing feelings of overwhelm and anxiety.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots are rich in antioxidants and have anti-inflammatory properties, making them an effective ingredient for calming anxiety and stress.
Flowers: Lavender flowers are the most commonly used part of the plant for their calming effects on the nervous system, helping to reduce stress and promote relaxation.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 10 pounds of fresh lavender flowers at peak bloom in the morning when dew is still present.
Dry the harvested flowers for 3-4 days indoors away from direct sunlight and moisture.
Crush the dried lavender flowers into a fine powder using a mortar and pestle or food processor.
Steam distill 1 pound of crushed lavender powder in 10 pounds of water at 100°c for 2 hours.
Collect and filter the resulting essential oil through a coffee filter or paper towels to remove impurities.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Lemon balm essential oils helps with stress because it contains compounds like rosmarinic acid, which has a natural calming effect on the mind and body. When inhaled or applied topically, lemon balm oil can help reduce feelings of anxiety and tension, promoting relaxation and serenity. Its sweet, citrusy aroma also has a mood-boosting effect, helping to uplift and calm the spirit. As a result, lemon balm essential oils are often used as a natural remedy for stress relief and promoting a good night's sleep.
remedy_properties: Anxiolytic properties: Herbal lemon balm essential oil has anxiolytic properties, which means it helps to calm the mind and body, reducing feelings of anxiety and stress.
Antioxidant activity: The antioxidants present in lemon balm essential oil help to neutralize free radicals, which can contribute to stress-related inflammation and oxidative damage.

GABA-enhancing effects: Lemon balm essential oil has been shown to enhance the activity of GABA (gamma-aminobutyric acid), a neurotransmitter that helps regulate relaxation and calmness, making it easier to manage stress and anxiety.
remedy_parts: Leaves: The leaves of the lemon balm plant are often used in aromatherapy due to their calming and uplifting properties, making them an effective remedy for reducing anxiety and stress.
Flowers: Lemon balm flowers contain high concentrations of citral and geraniol, two compounds that have been shown to reduce stress and promote relaxation.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh melissa officinalis leaves and stems in the early morning or late afternoon.
Wash the harvested plant material gently with cold water to remove dirt and debris.
Dry the washed plant material using a clean towel or air dryer for 1-2 hours.
Steam distill the dried plant material using a still or steam distillation apparatus at 100°c.
Collect and filter the resulting essential oil through cheesecloth or a coffee filter within 24 hours.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Chamomile essential oils helps with stress because of its calming and soothing properties. When inhaled, chamomile oil can slow down a racing mind and promote relaxation by targeting the amygdala, the brain's emotional center. It also has a sedative effect on the nervous system, which can help reduce anxiety and tension. Additionally, chamomile oil can calm the body's physiological response to stress, such as rapid heartbeat and increased blood pressure, allowing individuals to feel more centered and composed in times of turmoil.
remedy_properties: Anxiolytic (anxiety-relieving) property: Chamomile essential oil has been shown to reduce anxiety and stress by promoting relaxation and calming the mind and body.
Antispasmodic property: Chamomile essential oil's ability to relax muscle spasms and ease tension can help alleviate physical symptoms of stress, such as headaches and stomachaches.
Sedative property: Chamomile essential oil's sedative properties can promote a sense of calmness and relaxation, helping to reduce stress and anxiety by slowing down the nervous system.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in apigenin, an antioxidant that helps to calm and soothe the mind and body.
Flowers: Containing azulene, a potent anti-inflammatory compound that promotes relaxation and reduces anxiety.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh chamomilla flowers, collecting approximately 500 grams for distillation.
Wash the chamomilla flowers gently with cold water to remove dirt and debris.
Steam distill the cleaned chamomilla flowers using a still or apparatus for 2-3 hours.
Collect the extracted essential oil by condensing the steam vapor into a glass container, yielding approximately 1 ounce of oil.
Filter the essential oil through a coffee filter to remove any impurities and sediment, resulting in a clear golden liquid.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Lime essential oils helps with stress because of its calming and uplifting properties. The citrus scent has a way of lifting your mood, reducing anxiety, and promoting relaxation. When inhaled, it can slow down your heart rate and lower blood pressure, allowing you to unwind and feel more centered. Additionally, lime oil contains natural antioxidants that help combat free radicals, which can contribute to feelings of stress and fatigue. By incorporating herbal lime essential oils into your daily routine, you can experience a sense of calm and clarity in just a few minutes.
remedy_properties: Adaptogenic Properties: Herbal lime essential oil helps the body adapt to stress by regulating cortisol levels, promoting a sense of balance and calmness, and reducing anxiety.

Anxiolytic (Anti-Anxiety) Properties: The oil's anxiolytic properties help reduce feelings of worry, apprehension, and fear, promoting relaxation and reducing stress-related symptoms such as rapid heartbeat and sweating.

Uplifting and Mood-Enhancing Properties: The uplifting and mood-enhancing properties of herbal lime essential oil help to improve one's emotional state by releasing endorphins, which are natural mood-elevators that can counteract the negative effects of stress.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in antioxidants and adaptogenic compounds, lime roots help calm the nervous system and reduce anxiety.
Barks: Lime barks contain anti-inflammatory and analgesic properties, making them effective in soothing stress-related pain and discomfort.
remedy_recipe: Harvest high-quality tilia x europaea flowers during full bloom.
Dry the fresh flowers in a warm dark place for at least 7 days.
Steam distill the dried flowers using 10 kg of flowers and 2 liters of water.
Separate the essential oil from the hydrosol by decantation after 30 minutes.
Filter the essential oil through a coffee filter paper to remove any impurities.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Valerian essential oils helps with stress because it has a calming effect on the mind and body. The sedative properties of valerian root extract, found in high concentrations in these oils, work to quiet a racing mind and relax tense muscles. As the body's natural response to stress is reduced, feelings of anxiety and overwhelm dissipate, replaced by a sense of calm and tranquility. This allows individuals to better cope with stressful situations and promotes a restful night's sleep, further reducing overall stress levels.
remedy_properties: Anxiolytic (anxiety-reducing) property: Valerian essential oil has a calming effect on the nervous system, which helps to reduce anxiety and promote relaxation.
Sedative property: The sedative effects of valerian essential oil can help to slow down an overactive mind and promote a sense of calmness, making it easier to fall asleep and stay asleep.
Adaptogenic property: Valerian essential oil helps the body adapt to stress by reducing cortisol levels, promoting balance in the nervous system, and supporting overall resilience.
remedy_parts: Roots: Valerian roots are a rich source of sesquiterpenes, which have been shown to have a calming effect on the nervous system and can help reduce anxiety and stress.
Barks: The barks of the valerian plant contain valerenic acid, a compound that has been found to have a sedative effect and can help alleviate symptoms of insomnia and restlessness caused by stress.
remedy_recipe: Harvest high-quality valeriana officinalis roots and flowers in the morning after the dew has dried.
Dry the harvested plant material using a food dehydrator on low heat for 24 hours.
Steam distill the dried valeriana officinalis roots and flowers at 100°c with a 10:1 ratio of water to plant material.
Collect the essential oil that separates from the distilled steam and discard the remaining plant material.
Transfer the collected essential oil to dark glass bottles filled to the brim for storage and use.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Maypop essential oils helps with stress because of its calming and sedative properties, which can help to slow down a racing mind and promote relaxation. The oil's botanical composition, rich in linalool and limonene, works to reduce anxiety and cortisol levels, creating a sense of serenity and tranquility. When inhaled or applied topically, maypop essential oil can also stimulate the production of feel-good hormones like serotonin and dopamine, helping to alleviate symptoms of stress and promote a state of well-being.
remedy_properties: Anxiolytic properties: Maypop contains flavonoids and glycosides that have been shown to exhibit anxiolytic (anxiety-reducing) effects, which could help alleviate stress.
Adaptogenic effects: Maypop has been traditionally used as an adaptogen, meaning it may help the body adapt to stress by normalizing cortisol levels and promoting a sense of calm and balance.

GABA-enhancing properties: Maypop contains compounds that have been shown to increase GABA (gamma-aminobutyric acid) levels in the brain, which is a neurotransmitter that helps regulate relaxation and reduce anxiety.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of the Maypop plant contain sesquiterpenes, which have been shown to promote relaxation and reduce anxiety when inhaled.
Fruits: Maypop fruits are rich in flavonoids, which can help to calm the nervous system and reduce stress when used as a therapeutic essential oil.
remedy_recipe: Harvest high-quality passiflora incarnata flowers in the morning after dew has evaporated.
Dry the flowers using air circulation or machine drying at 40°c for 24 hours.
Steam distill the dried flowers with 0.5 grams of material per 100ml of distilled water for 30 minutes.
Cool the essential oil and separate it from the hydrosol by decantation or filtration within 2 hours.
Store the passiflora incarnata essential oil in a dark glass bottle at room temperature and use within 6 months.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Asiatic pennywort essential oils helps with stress because of its calming and sedative properties, which promote relaxation and reduce anxiety. The oil's natural compounds, such as alkaloids and glycosides, have been shown to have a positive impact on the nervous system, helping to calm the mind and body. By reducing feelings of tension and overwhelm, Asiatic pennywort essential oils can help individuals feel more centered and focused, making it an excellent addition to stress management routines.
remedy_properties: Antioxidant properties: Asiatic Pennywort Essential Oil has antioxidant properties that help protect the body from free radical damage caused by stress, which can lead to chronic diseases.
Adaptogenic properties: The oil helps the body adapt to stressful situations by normalizing cortisol levels and promoting a sense of balance and calmness, reducing anxiety and fatigue.

Anti-anxiety properties: Asiatic Pennywort Essential Oil has been shown to have anti-anxiety effects by interacting with GABA receptors in the brain, which can help reduce feelings of tension and stress.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in triterpenoids and flavonoids, which help to calm the nervous system and reduce anxiety.
Rhyzomes: Contain sesquiterpenes that promote relaxation and soothe muscle tension.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh centella asiatica leaves from a clean environment and rinse them thoroughly with distilled water.
Chop the leaves into small pieces and combine with 20 grams of carrier oil in a clean glass jar.
Steep the mixture in a dark place at room temperature for 4 weeks shaking the jar daily.
Strain the mixture through a coffee filter or cheesecloth into another glass container discarding solids.
Store the resulting essential oil in a dark glass bottle with an airtight cap and use within 6 months.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Hawthorn essential oils helps with stress because of its calming and sedative properties, which promote relaxation and reduce feelings of anxiety. The oil's ability to soothe the nervous system allows individuals to feel more grounded and centered, making it easier to manage stressful situations. Additionally, hawthorn's natural adaptogenic properties help to regulate the body's response to stress, promoting a sense of balance and well-being. By incorporating hawthorn essential oils into their daily routine, individuals can experience a significant reduction in stress levels and an overall improvement in their mental health.
remedy_properties: Antioxidant properties: Hawthorn essential oil contains powerful antioxidants that help neutralize free radicals, reducing oxidative stress and inflammation caused by chronic stress, which can contribute to anxiety and depression.

Adaptogenic properties: Hawthorn essential oil has been shown to have adaptogenic effects, helping the body adapt to stress by normalizing blood pressure, heart rate, and cortisol levels, promoting feelings of calmness and relaxation.

Sedative and anxiolytic properties: The constituents in hawthorn essential oil, such as flavonoids and terpenes, have been found to possess sedative and anxiolytic effects, helping to reduce anxiety, promote relaxation, and improve sleep quality, which are all important factors in managing stress.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in bioactive compounds that help reduce anxiety and promote relaxation, making them a prime candidate for stress-relieving essential oils.

Barks: Containing flavonoids and phenolic acids, Hawthorn barks have been traditionally used to calm the nervous system and ease tension, making them an excellent choice for stress-reducing essential oils.
remedy_recipe: Harvest high-quality crataegus monogyna leaves in early summer when they are at their peak potency for stress relief.
Dry the harvested leaves using a food dehydrator on low heat for 12 hours to preserve their beneficial compounds.
Steam distill the dried leaves in a still apparatus with distilled water at a ratio of 1:5 for 4 hours to extract the essential oil.
Filter the resulting essential oil through a coffee filter or cotton cloth to remove any impurities or sediment, yielding approximately 0.5ml of oil per 100g of plant material.
Store the extracted crataegus monogyna essential oil in dark glass bottles with tight-fitting lids and keep it away from direct sunlight and heat for future use.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: St John's wort essential oils helps with stress because it possesses a unique blend of constituents that work together to promote relaxation and calmness. The oil contains hyperforin, which has been shown to interact with neurotransmitters in the brain, reducing anxiety and fear responses. Additionally, its anti-inflammatory properties help to reduce cortisol levels, alleviating feelings of tension and overwhelm. As a result, St John's wort essential oils can be used aromatically or topically to calm the mind and body, promoting a sense of peace and tranquility.
remedy_properties: Antioxidant: St. John's Wort essential oil has antioxidant properties, which help protect the body from oxidative stress caused by free radicals, contributing to feelings of calmness and reducing anxiety.

Adaptogenic: The adaptogenic properties of St. John's Wort essential oil allow it to help the body adapt to stress, promoting a sense of balance and equilibrium, and reducing the negative effects of stress on the mind and body.
Mood-Enhancing: The oil contains compounds that have been shown to increase serotonin levels in the brain, which helps regulate mood, reducing symptoms of depression, anxiety, and stress.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in sesquiterpenes and flavonoids, roots of St John's Wort are valued for their adaptogenic properties, helping to alleviate stress and anxiety.
Flowers: The flowers of St John's Wort contain a high concentration of hyperforin, a compound known for its ability to reduce stress and promote relaxation.
remedy_recipe: Harvest high-quality fresh or dried st john's wort flowers and leaves in the morning when they are most fragrant.
Dry the plant material using a dehydrator on low heat for 24 hours to preserve its medicinal properties.
Chop the dried plant material into small pieces and pack it tightly into a clean glass jar with a tight-fitting lid.
Fill a slow cooker with water and add the chopped st john's wort mixture, covering it by at least an inch, and let it steep for 6 hours.

Strain the liquid through a cheesecloth or coffee filter into a clean glass container, discarding the solids, and allow it to cool before transferring it to dark glass bottles with tight-fitting lids for storage.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Skullcap essential oils helps with stress because of its natural adaptogenic properties, which allow it to regulate and balance the body's response to stressors. When inhaled or applied topically, skullcap essential oil can help calm the mind and body, reducing feelings of anxiety and tension. Its soothing aroma also promotes relaxation and serenity, making it an effective natural remedy for managing stress and promoting overall well-being.
remedy_properties: Anxiolytic property: Skullcap essential oil has been shown to possess anxiolytic (anti-anxiety) effects, helping to reduce feelings of stress and anxiety by interacting with GABA receptors in the brain.
Muscle relaxant property: The essential oil has a calming effect on muscle tissue, which can help alleviate physical tension associated with stress, promoting relaxation and reducing muscle spasms.

Sedative property: Skullcap essential oil contains compounds that have a sedative effect, helping to promote relaxation, reduce restlessness, and improve sleep quality, all of which are compromised by chronic stress.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of the skullcap plant are rich in sesquiterpenes, which have a calming effect on the nervous system, making them ideal for reducing stress and anxiety.
Leaves: The leaves contain flavonoids and phenolic acids that have anti-inflammatory properties, helping to soothe and calm the mind and body during times of stress.
remedy_recipe: Harvest high-quality scutellaria baicalensis leaves in the early morning after dew has evaporated, collecting approximately 100g of fresh material.
Dry the harvested scutellaria baicalensis leaves using a food dehydrator at 35°c for 24 hours or until completely dry and brittle.
Grind the dried scutellaria baicalensis leaves into a fine powder using a spice grinder, ensuring uniform particle size and approximately 20g of powdered material remains.
Combine the ground scutellaria baicalensis powder with carrier oil such as sweet almond oil in a 1:10 ratio, stirring well to ensure thorough blending and adding approximately 200ml of carrier oil.

Allow the mixture to steep for 2 weeks, shaking the container daily, before straining through cheesecloth or a coffee filter to extract the essential oil, resulting in approximately 20ml of scutellaria baicalensis essential oil.

supplementary_best_treatment: The best way to treat stress with herbal essential oils is by blending calming and grounding aromas. Use a combination of lavender, bergamot, and ylang-ylang essential oils to promote relaxation and reduce anxiety. Apply the blend topically to pulse points or inhale it through steam inhalation to alleviate stress and tension. This natural remedy can also be added to bath water for a calming and rejuvenating experience.
supplementary_causes: Ailments similar to stress that are treated with herbal essential oils are anxiety, fatigue, insomnia, and mood disorders. For example, Lavender oil is calming and relaxing for anxiety and insomnia, while Peppermint oil can help alleviate mental fogginess and improve focus. Frankincense oil has anti-inflammatory properties that can help reduce symptoms of depression, while Bergamot oil's uplifting scent can boost mood and reduce stress.

remedies.remedy_desc: Lavandula angustifolia essential oils helps with stress because of its calming and sedative properties. The oil contains linalool and linalyl acetate, which have a soothing effect on the nervous system, promoting relaxation and reducing anxiety. It has been traditionally used to calm the mind and body, helping individuals to unwind and prepare for sleep. Additionally, the oil's sweet, floral scent can help to calm the senses, making it an effective tool for managing stress and promoting overall well-being.
remedy_constituents: Linalool: Acts as a natural anxiolytic by reducing anxiety and stress levels due to its sedative properties.
Linalyl acetate: Exhibits a calming effect by modulating the body's stress response and promoting relaxation.
Caryophyllene oxide: Helps reduce stress by exerting a sedative effect and promoting a sense of calmness through its interaction with the body's endocannabinoid system.
remedy_parts: Leaves: The leaves are often used to make essential oils for stress due to their calming properties and ability to promote relaxation.
Flowers: The flowers are commonly used to make essential oils for stress as they contain high levels of linalool, a compound known for its anxiety-reducing and calming effects.
Stems: The stems of Lavandula angustifolia are also used to make essential oils for stress, as they contain similar compounds to the flowers and leaves, contributing to their calming properties.
remedy_recipe: Harvest approximately 1000g of fresh lavandula angustifolia flowers in the early morning when the dew is still present.
Steam distill the harvested flowers at a temperature range of 40-60°c for 2-3 hours with 10kg of water.
Collect the distilled essential oil and separate it from the water and plant material through decantation.
Store the collected essential oil in a dark glass bottle with a tight-fitting lid away from direct sunlight.
Allow the essential oil to mature for 2-3 weeks before using it for its therapeutic benefits.

remedies.remedy_desc: Valeriana officinalis essential oils helps with stress because it has been traditionally used to promote relaxation and calmness. The oil's sedative properties work to reduce anxiety and promote a peaceful state, making it easier to fall asleep and improve overall sleep quality. The calming effects of Valeriana officinalis essential oils also help to reduce muscle tension and promote a sense of well-being, allowing individuals to manage stress and feel more balanced and centered.
remedy_constituents: Valerenic acid: A valerenic acid derivative helps reduce stress and anxiety by interacting with the GABA receptor, promoting relaxation and calming effects.
Valeranone: Valeranone has a sedative effect that aids in stress relief by reducing the activity of the central nervous system, leading to a calming and soothing impact.

Valeranols: Valeranols act as a natural stress-reducing agent by inhibiting the activity of the enzyme acetylcholinesterase, which in turn, helps regulate the neurotransmitter acetylcholine and promotes relaxation.
remedy_parts: Roots: Valerian roots contain valerenic acid, which is responsible for their sedative properties, making them a key ingredient in essential oils for stress relief.
Flowers: Valerian flowers are used to create essential oils that promote relaxation and reduce anxiety due to their high concentration of valerenal and other calming compounds.
Leaves: Valerian leaves are another part of the plant used to make essential oils that help alleviate stress and promote sleep due to their content of valerenic acid and other soothing compounds.
remedy_recipe: Harvest mature roots of valeriana officinalis at dawn or before noon for better quality.
Dry the harvested roots in a warm place with good air circulation for 2 weeks.
Steam distill the dried roots using 1 part roots to 2 parts water at 70-80 degrees celsius.
Collect the essential oil and filter it through a coffee filter paper to remove impurities.
Store the extracted essential oil in a dark glass bottle with a tight-fitting lid immediately.

remedies.remedy_desc: Melissa officinalis essential oils helps with stress because of its calming and soothing properties. The oil is extracted from the leaves of the lemon balm plant, which has been traditionally used to calm the mind and body. The high levels of linalool and beta-caryophyllene in Melissa officinalis essential oils promote relaxation, reduce anxiety, and improve mood. By reducing stress and anxiety, Melissa officinalis essential oils help to promote a sense of calm and well-being, making it an effective natural remedy for managing stress and promoting a good night's sleep.
remedy_constituents: Linalool: Acts as a natural anxiolytic by reducing anxiety and stress levels through its sedative and calming effects on the nervous system.
Borneol: Exhibits a calming effect on the mind and body by reducing stress and anxiety through its ability to regulate the nervous system and promote relaxation.
Geranial: Demonstrates a stress-reducing effect by promoting a sense of calmness and relaxation, which helps alleviate anxiety and stress-related symptoms.
remedy_parts: Leaves: They contain the highest concentration of citral, a key compound responsible for the calming and relaxing properties of the oil.
Stems: They contribute to the oil's antimicrobial properties, which help to reduce stress and promote overall well-being.
Flowers: They are rich in linalool, a compound that has been shown to have a calming effect on the nervous system and promote relaxation.
remedy_recipe: Harvest melissa leaves at dawn in late spring or early summer when they are in full bloom and highly fragrant.
Steam distill 3 kg of melissa leaves in 3 liters of distilled water at 100°c for 2-3 hours.
Separate the essential oil from the distillate through decantation and filter it using a coffee filter paper.
Store the essential oil in a dark glass bottle with a tight-fitting lid in a cool dry place for 6 months.
Label and date the bottle and keep it away from children and pets to ensure safe storage.

remedies.remedy_desc: Passiflora incarnata essential oils helps with stress because it promotes relaxation and calms the mind. The oil's anxiolytic properties soothe anxiety and reduce feelings of overwhelm. By regulating the body's response to stress, it encourages a sense of tranquility and balance. Additionally, its sedative effects help to quiet the mind, allowing individuals to unwind and recharge. As a result, Passiflora incarnata essential oils can be a valuable tool in managing stress and promoting overall well-being.
remedy_constituents: Isovitalexin B2: This constituent has been shown to have anxiolytic properties, helping to reduce anxiety and stress by interacting with the body's GABA receptors.
Flavonoids (specifically, kaempferol and quercetin): These polyphenolic compounds possess antioxidant properties, which can help mitigate oxidative stress and promote relaxation in the body.
Methylflavonol glycosides: These compounds have been found to have a sedative effect, helping to calm the nervous system and reduce stress and anxiety.
remedy_parts: Leaves: They are rich in flavonoids and other compounds that have a calming effect on the nervous system.
Flowers: Their essential oil is known for its anxiolytic properties, which help alleviate stress and anxiety.
Stems: The stems of the plant contain a high concentration of flavonoids, which contribute to their stress-relieving properties when extracted into essential oil.
remedy_recipe: Harvest the passiflora incarnata flowers at dawn when dew is still present on the plant's surface.
Steam distillation is performed using 100g of fresh flowers in a 500ml still for 4 hours.
Collect the distilled essential oil and separate it from the condensed water through decantation.
Filter the essential oil using a 5 micron filter paper to remove any particulate matter.
Store the passiflora incarnata essential oil in a dark glass bottle sealed with a tight fitting stopper.

remedies.remedy_desc: Anthemis nobilis essential oils helps with stress because of its calming and soothing properties. Derived from the flowers of the chamomile plant, this oil has a gentle and relaxing effect on the mind and body. Its anti-inflammatory and antioxidant compounds help to reduce anxiety and promote a sense of tranquility. By promoting a restful night's sleep and calming the nervous system, Anthemis nobilis essential oils can effectively alleviate stress, promoting a more balanced and peaceful state of being.
remedy_constituents: Linalool: Acts as a natural sedative and anxiolytic, helping to reduce anxiety and promote relaxation.
Linalyl acetate: Exhibits a calming effect on the nervous system, reducing stress and promoting a sense of calm.
Borneol: Possesses a calming and sedative effect, helping to reduce stress and anxiety by promoting relaxation.
remedy_parts: Flowers: Used due to their soothing and calming properties, which help reduce anxiety and stress.
Leaves: Utilized for their mild sedative and calming effects, which aid in stress relief and relaxation.
Rhyzomes: Employed for their antispasmodic and anti-inflammatory properties, which help alleviate stress-related symptoms and promote relaxation.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 50g of fresh anthemis nobilis flowers in the early morning to ensure optimal oil content.
Steam distill the harvested flowers for 1 hour at 100-110°c to extract essential oil.
Collect the distilled oil and separate it from water using a separatory funnel.
Filter the collected essential oil through a 0.2 micron filter paper to remove impurities.
Store the filtered essential oil in a dark glass bottle with a tight-fitting lid at 15-20°c.

remedies.remedy_desc: Santalum album essential oils helps with stress because it possesses a unique blend of properties that promote relaxation and calmness. The oil's woody and earthy aroma has a grounding effect, helping to quiet the mind and reduce anxiety. Its sedative and anti-inflammatory properties also work to soothe the body, reducing muscle tension and promoting a sense of balance and well-being. As a result, Santalum album essential oils can help individuals unwind and find peace in a world filled with stress and uncertainty.
remedy_constituents: α-Santalol: Helps with stress by promoting relaxation and reducing anxiety through its sedative properties.
β-Santalol: Contributes to stress relief by exerting a calming effect on the mind and body due to its anxiolytic properties.
Santalenic acid: Assists in stress management by acting as an anti-inflammatory agent and reducing oxidative stress, which can contribute to anxiety and other stress-related issues.
remedy_parts: Rhyzomes: Rhyzomes are the most commonly used part of Santalum album for essential oil production due to their high concentration of Santalol, a compound known for its calming and relaxing properties.

Barks: The barks of Santalum album are also widely used for essential oil production, as they contain a significant amount of Santalol and are believed to have a grounding and calming effect on the mind and body.

Buds: The buds of Santalum album are sometimes used for essential oil production, as they contain a high concentration of Santalol and are thought to have a soothing and stress-relieving effect on the body and mind.
remedy_recipe: Harvest santalum album leaves and branches at dawn when the dew is still present to ensure freshness.
Steam distill the harvested plant material at 100 degrees celsius for 2 hours to release the essential oils.
Collect the distilled water and essential oils from the steam distillation process and separate them using a separator.
Filter the essential oils through a charcoal filter to remove impurities and improve their quality.
Store the filtered santalum album essential oil in a dark glass bottle with a tight-fitting lid to preserve its potency.

remedies.remedy_desc: Avena sativa essential oils helps with stress because it contains natural sedative and anti-anxiety properties, promoting relaxation and calming the mind. The oil's soothing effects can help reduce feelings of overwhelm and anxiety, allowing the body to unwind and recharge. By balancing the nervous system and promoting a sense of calm, Avena sativa essential oils support the body's natural response to stress, making it an effective natural remedy for promoting relaxation and reducing stress levels.
remedy_constituents: Beta-Amylase Inhibitor: This constituent helps with stress by reducing anxiety and promoting relaxation by inhibiting the activity of beta-amylase, an enzyme involved in the breakdown of starch into sugars, which can contribute to feelings of stress and anxiety.

Lignans: Lignans in Avena sativa essential oils help with stress by exerting a sedative effect, promoting relaxation, and reducing anxiety due to their ability to interact with the body's endocrine system.

Coumarins: Coumarins in Avena sativa essential oils help with stress by promoting relaxation and reducing anxiety through their ability to modulate the body's response to stress, promoting a sense of calmness and well-being.
remedy_parts: Flowers: They are used for their calming and soothing properties, which help in reducing stress and anxiety.
Seeds: They are used for their sedative and anti-anxiety effects, which aid in promoting relaxation and reducing stress.
Leaves: They are used for their adaptogenic properties, which help the body to adapt to stress and promote balance.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 1-2 pounds of avena sativa flowers at peak bloom for optimal oil content and potency.
Steam distill the flowers at a temperature of 100-150 degrees fahrenheit for 2-3 hours.
Collect the distilled oil and filter it through a paper filter or cheesecloth to remove impurities.
Store the filtered oil in a dark glass bottle with a tight-fitting lid to preserve its quality.
Use the essential oil immediately or within 6-12 months of preparation for best results.

remedies.remedy_desc: Boswellia serrata essential oils helps with stress because it possesses anti-inflammatory and adaptogenic properties that promote relaxation and calmness. The oil's bioactive compounds, such as boswellic acids, interact with the body's nervous system to reduce anxiety and cortisol levels. By calming the mind and body, Boswellia serrata essential oils facilitate a sense of balance and tranquility, making it an effective natural remedy for stress relief and promoting overall well-being. It also has a grounding effect, helping to stabilize emotions.
remedy_constituents: Terpenoids (Boswellic Acids): They help with stress by inhibiting the production of pro-inflammatory enzymes and cytokines, which contribute to stress-induced inflammation and anxiety.
Furanosesquiterpenes: They help with stress by exhibiting anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which can help alleviate stress-induced oxidative stress and inflammation in the body.

Phenolic Compounds: They help with stress by exhibiting antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties, which can help reduce stress-induced oxidative stress and inflammation, and promote relaxation and calmness.
remedy_parts: Roots: Boswellia serrata roots are commonly used to make essential oils for stress due to their high concentration of boswellic acids, which have anti-inflammatory and anxiolytic properties.

Rhyzomes: Boswellia serrata rhyzomes are used to make essential oils for stress due to their rich content of essential oils, including alpha-pinene and beta-pinene, which have calming and anti-anxiety effects.
Barks: Boswellia serrata barks are used to make essential oils for stress due to their ability to reduce stress and anxiety by regulating the body's response to stress hormones.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 10 kg of boswellia serrata resin from mature trees at the peak of the summer season.
Steam distill the harvested resin for 6 hours using a 1:3 resin to water ratio.
Collect the distillate and separate the essential oil from water using a separating funnel.
Filter the essential oil through a 0.2 micron filter paper to remove impurities and improve clarity.
Store the filtered essential oil in a dark glass bottle at room temperature for up to 2 years.

remedies.remedy_desc: Matricaria chamomilla essential oils helps with stress because it promotes relaxation and calms the mind. The soothing properties of chamomile oil have been used for centuries to alleviate anxiety and promote a good night's sleep. The oil's anti-inflammatory properties also help to reduce muscle tension and promote a sense of calmness. As a result, chamomile essential oil can help individuals unwind and cope with stress, making it a popular choice for aromatherapy and stress relief techniques. Regular use can lead to improved overall well-being.
remedy_constituents: Matricine: Acts as a potent anxiolytic, reducing anxiety and stress responses by modulating the central nervous system.
Apigenin: Exerts anti-stress effects by interacting with GABA receptors, promoting relaxation and reducing anxiety symptoms.
Bisabolol: Exhibits a calming effect on the nervous system, reducing stress and anxiety by promoting a sense of relaxation and tranquility.
remedy_parts: Flowers: Used because they contain high amounts of chamazulene, an anti-inflammatory compound that helps to calm and soothe the mind and body.
Leaves: Used because they have a high concentration of apigenin, an antioxidant that helps to reduce anxiety and promote relaxation.
Stems: Not commonly used.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh matricaria chamomilla flowers at dawn when dew is still present on the plant for best quality.
Steam distill the harvested flowers with 0.2% water content at 105 degrees fahrenheit for 2 hours.
Collect the distilled vapors that rise from the distillation apparatus and separate from water.
Store the collected essential oil in a dark glass bottle with a tight lid at room temperature.
Filter the essential oil through a paper filter before use to remove any impurities that may have formed.

remedies.remedy_desc: Bacopa monnieri essential oils helps with stress because it promotes relaxation and calms the mind. The oil's active compounds, such as bacosides, have been shown to have a positive impact on the brain's neurotransmitters, reducing anxiety and stress levels. It also has a sedative effect, allowing individuals to unwind and reduce feelings of overwhelm. By promoting a sense of calm and tranquility, Bacopa monnieri essential oils can help alleviate stress and promote a better night's sleep, leading to improved overall well-being.
remedy_constituents: Bacosides: Bacosides have been shown to exhibit adaptogenic properties, helping to reduce stress and anxiety by regulating the body's response to stress.
Monnierin: Monnierin has been found to possess antioxidant and neuroprotective properties, which can help mitigate the negative effects of stress on the brain and nervous system.
Bacopaside: Bacopaside has been reported to have anxiolytic and anti-depressant properties, helping to alleviate symptoms of stress and anxiety by promoting a sense of calm and well-being.
remedy_parts: Leaves: The leaves are commonly used due to their high concentration of bacosides, which are believed to have a positive effect on cognitive function and stress relief.
Stems: The stems are utilized for their bacoside content, which may help alleviate stress and anxiety.
Roots: The roots are often used in essential oils to take advantage of their bacoside-rich properties, potentially aiding in stress reduction and improved memory.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 20-30 grams of fresh bacopa monnieri leaves and flowers at dawn when the plant is in full bloom.
Steam distill the plant material in a 2-liter distillation apparatus for 2-3 hours at 100-110 degrees celsius.
Collect the distilled essential oil and separate it from the water using a separatory funnel.
Filter the essential oil through a coffee filter or a 0.2-micron filter paper to remove impurities.
Store the final herbal bacopa monnieri essential oil in a dark glass bottle with a tight-fitting lid at room temperature.
